t_ch: 只是去兼职，矿场的工作当然不会耽误啦。反正坐缆车通勤也花不了多长时间，我已经做好心理准备了。
t_en: I'll only be working part-time, so it won't affect my job at the mine. It doesn't take long to commute by cable car anyway. I'm mentally prepared for it.

t_ch: 每天都去上层区？不累吗？
t_en: Wouldn't it be tiring to travel to the Overworld daily?

t_ch: 我倒是考虑过这个问题…不过坐缆车通勤也花不了多长时间嘛，我已经做好心理准备啦。
t_en: I've considered the issue. It doesn't take long to commute by cable car and I'm mentally prepared for it.

t_ch: 总之，现在就等上下层区正式开放咯，不知道能找到什么样的工作呢？啊，走之前还得和歌蒂师傅说一声……
t_en: Anyway, I'm just waiting for the Over and Underworlds to open up. I wonder what kind of job I'll be able to land. Oh, I should talk to Master Gertie before I leave.

t_ch: 假如一直低着头行走，每一步都害怕陷进泥潭…我们的脚步一定会越发沉重，士气也会变得低迷。
t_en: If you walk with your head down and your heart full of fear, your steps will grow heavier and your spirit will suffer.

t_ch: 如果我们找到了某样值得仰望的东西…满怀希望地上路，步子都会变得轻盈，就算被绊倒了也能很快振作起来，重新出发。
t_en: But if you find something to look up to and move forward with hope, then your steps will grow lighter... and if you should fall, you'll be on your feet before you know it.

t_ch: ——谢谢你们教会了我这些，开拓者。
t_en: ...Thank you for teaching me this, Trailblazers.

t_ch: 有时间聊聊吗，布洛妮娅？
t_en: Do you have time to chat, Bronya?

t_ch: 当然有，{NICKNAME}。和你谈话总能让我受益良多。
t_en: Of course, I do, {NICKNAME}. I've always benefited lots from our discussions.

t_ch: 当上「大守护者」的感觉如何？
t_en: How does it feel to be the Supreme Guardian?

t_ch: 于我而言，「大守护者」不过是个称号，仅此而已。不管有没有这个头衔，我都会坚持做正确的事。
t_en: For me, it's just a title and nothing more. I'll still stick to doing the right thing with or without this title.

t_ch: …但站在民众的角度来看则不然。「大守护者」在他们眼中是一个分量极重的符号——它象征的不仅是城市的领袖，更是未来的希望。
t_en: ...But not from the point of view of the people. For them, the Supreme Guardian is a powerful symbol — not just of the city's leadership, but also of a future with hope.

t_ch: 所以，我必须更加严格地审视自己的思想。必须时刻记住，我的每一个决策都可能影响成百上千人的生活……
t_en: So I have to re-examine my thinking more critically. I have to always bear in mind that every decision I make could affect the lives of thousands of people...

t_ch: 这个担子很重。实话说，我也不知道自己能否胜任。但我很幸运——因为我不需要独自承受这一切。
t_en: It's a heavy load to bear. To be honest, I don't even know if I can do it. But I'm lucky — because I don't have to go through it all alone.

t_ch: 接下来有什么计划？
t_en: What plans do you have next?

t_ch: 头等大事当然是振兴贝洛伯格…啊，听上去还是太笼统了，对吧？
t_en: The number one priority is to revitalize Belobog... Ah, it still sounds so vague, doesn't it?

t_ch: 落实到具体的行动上，我认为排在第一位的莫过于重新梳理贝洛伯格的经济环境。
t_en: As for specific actions, I think the number one priority is to reorganize the economy of Belobog.

t_ch: 封锁令对上下层区的财政状况造成了巨大的影响。眼下，普通的下层区矿民根本无法承受行政区的物价。
t_en: The blockade has had a huge impact on the Overworld and Underworld economies. Right now, ordinary miners in the Underworld can't bear the high cost of living in the Administrative District.

t_ch: 假如现状不能改变，那上下层区的再融合也就无从谈起。我已经安排了与几位财政大臣的会面，希望能商量出基本的改革方向。
t_en: If things don't get better, then you can forget about reintegrating the Overworld and the Underworld. I've booked meetings with several of the finance ministers to try to hammer out a basic direction for reforms.

t_ch: ——当然，这只是振兴计划的第一环。希望这能成为一个好的开始。
t_en: — Of course, this is only the first step in a great revitalization plan. I hope it will be a strong start.

t_ch: 关于可可利亚…
t_en: About Cocolia...

t_ch: …小声些，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: ...Keep your voice down, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 关于母亲…我只能尽力不去回想她临终前的样子。
t_en: As for Mom... All I can do is keep myself from remembering how she looked before she passed.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，不管她在你们眼里是什么形象，可可利亚大人都是我唯一的亲人。我之所以能成为今天的布洛妮娅…很大程度上归功于她的教导。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, no matter what she might look like to you, Madam Cocolia was my only living family. Everything that has made me into the person I am... I owe it to her teachings.

t_ch: 我不知道她是从何时开始踏入了歧途，但一直以来，她倾注在我身上的只有纯粹的爱…我不相信那也是「星核」干涉的结果。
t_en: I don't know when she started going astray, but this whole time, all she's ever done has been to shower me with love... And I don't think that's the Stellaron's influence.

t_ch: 希儿不在吗？
t_en: Is Seele here?

t_ch: 与其杵在这死气沉沉的办公室里，她更想用自己的双手改变现状。何况她心里始终挂念着下层区的事，想强行留住也难。
t_en: She wants to get stuck into some material change, rather than stick around in this lifeless office. She was always thinking about the Underworld. You can't stop someone like that from leaving.

t_ch: 希儿是个行动派，她在互相推诿的会议桌上没法发挥自己的强项。她清楚自己最擅长什么…作为朋友、战友，我理应支持她的选择。
t_en: Seele is a doer. She can't do what she does best at the negotiation table. She knows where her strengths lie... And as a friend and fellow fighter, I support her choice.

t_ch: 况且，只要我发出需要帮助的讯号…我知道她一定会回到我身边。这样就够了。
t_en: Besides, if I send a signal asking for her help... I know she'll return to me. And that's all I need.

t_ch: 感谢你的时间，布洛妮娅。
t_en: Thanks for your time, Bronya.

t_ch: 再见，布洛妮娅。
t_en: Goodbye, Bronya.

t_ch: 再见，{NICKNAME}。祝愿你们的旅途一切顺利。
t_en: So long, {NICKNAME}. I wish you all the best on your journey.

t_ch: 你还在啊，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: You're still here, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 虽然现在的贝洛伯格千疮百孔…但只要观察得足够细致，就总能发现它迷人的一面。
t_en: Despite Belobog being in a terrible state these days... You can always find its charming side, if only you look carefully enough.

t_ch: 我相信你拥有一双能够发觉细微之美的眼睛，「开拓者」。希望你能在贝洛伯格度过一段美好的时光。
t_en: I trust you have a meticulous eye for beauty, Trailblazer. I hope you have a great time in Belobog.

t_ch: 不瞒你说，一想到我再也看不到她的面容…想大哭一场的冲动就会一个劲地涌上来。但我现在绝对不能表现出懦弱。
t_en: Honestly speaking, the thought of not seeing her again... always wells me up with tears. Yet, now is not the time for me to show my vulnerable side.

t_ch: 所以…我们换个话题吧，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: So, let's talk about something else, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 能聊聊和公司打交道的事吗？
t_en: Can we talk about dealings with the IPC?

t_ch: 当然可以——关于这件事，值得聊的内容可太多了…正好现在咱们都有空，那就好好展开说说吧。
t_en: Of course we can talk about that! There's much that is worth discussing... We're both free, what's on your mind?

t_ch: 关于托帕，你怎么看？
t_en: What do you think about Topaz?

t_ch: 托帕小姐…她很复杂。
t_en: Miss Topaz... She is very complex.

t_ch: 从她给我的信里，我能看到她真诚的一面；但她也会对我隐瞒重要的信息，用手段逼迫我们照她的思路行事。
t_en: From the letter she gave me, I could sense her sincerity. Yet she would also withhold important information from me, and coerce and manipulate us into doing what she desires.

t_ch: 但我能感受到，她的确是打心底在为这颗星球的未来着想——哪怕她坚持的方式无法被所有人接受。
t_en: However, I can feel that she is sincerely working for the future of this planet from the bottom of her heart, even if not everyone can accept her methods.

t_ch: 她是个有手腕的人。
t_en: She is very calculated.

t_ch: 没错，还好她能站在我们这边…这就叫因祸得福吧？贝洛伯格经历了一番重大的考验，但也因此获得了一位强大的盟友。
t_en: You're right. It's a relief that she is on our side... I guess this is what you'd call a blessing in disguise, right? Though Belobog experienced a great trial, it also gained a powerful ally because of it.

t_ch: 她是个值得信任的人。
t_en: She is a trustworthy person.

t_ch: 这一点，我跟你看法相同。但她所代表的势力，以及她展现出来的手腕，也让我们不得不心怀戒备。
t_en: This is something we agree on. Though the power that she represents and the calculated schemes that she has displayed have caused us to remain guarded.

t_ch: 幸好她现在能站在我们这边…这就叫因祸得福吧？贝洛伯格经历了一番重大的考验，但也因此获得了一位强大的盟友。
t_en: It's a relief that she is on our side now... I guess this is what you'd call a blessing in disguise, right? Though Belobog experienced a great trial, it also gained a powerful ally because of it.

t_ch: 但愿这能让「公司」暂时打消干预雅利洛-Ⅵ的念头。希望托帕小姐的承诺有足够的分量……
t_en: My wish is that this will make the IPC abandon their idea of interfering with Jarilo-VI. I hope Miss Topaz's promise carries enough weight...

t_ch: 你当时差点就签了合同。
t_en: You almost signed the contract back then.

t_ch: 没错…我当时正处在崩溃的边缘。幸好姬子小姐及时出现，带来了事件的全貌。
t_en: That's right... At that moment, I was on the verge of breaking down. Fortunately, Miss Himeko appeared in time and explained the whole picture.

t_ch: 我不知道托帕为什么没在信里提及那些数字和概率…是她对自己足够自信？还是她在刻意隐瞒？
t_en: I wonder why Topaz didn't mention the numbers and probability in her letter... Was she confident enough in herself? Or did she deliberately hide them from me?

t_ch: …但那都不重要了，我该反思的是自己当时的状态。我差点就被压力击倒了…在那个瞬间，我能想到的只有逃避责任、一了百了。
t_en: ...Well, that's no longer important. What I need to reflect on is how I felt at that moment, I almost caved to the pressure... At that moment, all I wanted was to escape my responsibilities, once and for all.

t_ch: 如果事情没有出现转机，我可能会辜负全城民众的信任…还有希儿的信任。我必须吸取教训，继续成长——我的意志必须更加坚定。
t_en: If things had not turned around, I may have betrayed the trust of all our citizens... and Seele's, too. I must learn from this lesson and continue to grow. My will must be stronger!

t_ch: 城里有很多公司的人。
t_en: There are many IPC people in the city.

t_ch: 对，是我同意让他们驻留在贝洛伯格的。
t_en: Indeed, I permitted them to station in Belobog.

t_ch: 留在这里的都是托帕小姐最信任的员工，他们似乎管这叫「技术支援类外派驻场」。
t_en: Those that are stationed here are all Topaz's most trusted employees. It seems they call this place an "Exo-Technical Support Dispatch Zone."

t_ch: 不管怎么说，我们一定用得上公司的技术。如果他们能安分守己地协助市民开展工作，贝洛伯格说不定能在一代人的时间里就完成他们口中的「技术跃迁」……
t_en: Whatever the case, we can definitely make use of the IPC's technology. If they can know their place and assist the citizens in their work, Belobog may even complete this "tech-leap" they speak of within a generation...

t_ch: 你能想象吗，{NICKNAME}？或许有朝一日——等我卸下大守护者的职责之后——我们真的可以并肩旅行。这不再是个白日梦了。
t_en: Can you imagine, {NICKNAME}? There may come a day — once I step down from my role as Supreme Guardian — that we could really set out to travel together. This is no longer just a pipe dream.

t_ch: 有关造物引擎和造物之柱…
t_en: About the Engine of Creation and Pillars of Creation...

t_ch: 诸位出发去找托帕以后，我立即通过贝洛伯格的城市广播进行了宣讲。
t_en: When you went in search of Topaz, I immediately made an appeal to the people on the Belobog city radio.

t_ch: 我将和公司相关的一连串事件背景都告知了民众，并呼吁他们拿起身边的工具，跟随铁卫一同前往北方，投入重铸造物引擎的工程。
t_en: I told the citizens everything behind the events surrounding the IPC, and urged them to pick up their tools and join the Silvermane guards northward, to direct their efforts toward the rebuilding of the Engine of Creation.

t_ch: 结果各位也看到了——不论背景出身、身份高低，上下层区的贝洛伯格人团结在了一起，为了维护这个世界的自由自主贡献力量。
t_en: And you all saw the outcome. The citizens of Belobog — regardless of background and status, both Underworlder and Overworlder — have all come together to contribute to maintaining the freedom and autonomy of this world.

t_ch: 我很高兴，{NICKNAME}…这一次我不必再编织话术，不必再为了安稳牺牲真诚。
t_en: I'm so happy, {NICKNAME}... I no longer have to concoct a lie this time. I don't have to sacrifice my sincerity for the sake of stability.

t_ch: 或许有那么一天…人们也能坦然地接受那个真相。
t_en: Perhaps, one day... people will also be able to calmly accept "that truth."

t_ch: 哟，{NICKNAME}。没想到你还没走啊——怎么，还在留恋贝洛伯格？
t_en: Hey, {NICKNAME}. I didn't expect you to still be here — don't tell me you've fallen in love with Belobog?

t_ch: 那倒没有…
t_en: Not really...

t_ch: …也是。你们去过那么多世界，肯定见过不少神奇的景色。贝洛伯格这巴掌大的地方，恐怕没什么吸引力吧？
t_en: ...Kinda. You've been all over the place and seen so many incredible sights. I bet a small place like Belobog has nothing to write home about, right?

t_ch: 确实是个好地方。
t_en: Really, it's a great town.

t_ch: 嘿，我还以为贝洛伯格这巴掌大的地方入不了你的法眼呢。
t_en: Hmm, I thought a small town like Belobog would be beneath someone like you.

t_ch: 贝洛伯格——下层区是我的家，我唯一熟悉的地方。有机会的话，我也想去见识见识外面的广阔世界…但绝对不是现在。
t_en: Belobog... the Underworld's my home, the only place I've ever known. If I get the chance, I'd love to see the world out there... But definitely not right now.

t_ch: 这里的人们需要帮助。我必须陪在他们身边，尽自己的一份力。
t_en: People here need help. I have to stay with them and do my part.

t_ch: 来聊聊吗，希儿？
t_en: Care to chat, Seele?

t_ch: 好啊，趁着现在有空——你想聊什么？
t_en: Okay, I'm free at the moment — What do you want to talk about?

t_ch: 你不待在布洛妮娅身边吗？
t_en: You're not with Bronya?

t_ch: 成天待在那座冰冰凉的大城堡里？放过我吧，我可习惯不了那种生活。
t_en: Staying all day cooped up in that ice castle? Spare me — I could never get used to that lifestyle.

t_ch: 真不知道布洛妮娅是怎么做到的——每天翻看那么多文件，听那么多人在耳边叨叨…换成我的话，迟早会疯掉。
t_en: I have no idea how Bronya does it — poring over all those documents every day, having to listen to all that idle chit-chat... I would've gone mad long ago.

t_ch: 我还是更适合户外的工作。况且，下层区还有很多我认识的人需要帮忙。这些想法我都跟布洛妮娅说了，她很支持。
t_en: I prefer working outside. Besides, there's a lot of people I know in the Underworld who need help. I've shared these thoughts with Bronya, and she's supportive.

t_ch: 当然，如果她需要我的话…我立马就会赶回去。即使站在她的位置，很多事情也必须借我之手解决…分工明确，没什么可抱怨的。
t_en: Of course, if she needs me... I'll be back in a snap. Even she needs somebody like me to help solve certain matters... We know our roles, and there's nothing to complain about.

t_ch: 下层区的状况如何？
t_en: How are things down in the Underworld?

t_ch: 不怎么样，不过已经有些好转的征兆了。
t_en: Not good — but there're some signs of improvement.

t_ch: 娜塔和奥列格头儿每天都在四处奔忙，帮忙协调上下层区的贸易重启。我对商业、经济什么的一窍不通，不想去给他们添倒忙。
t_en: Nat and Oleg run around every day, helping to coordinate getting trade started again. I don't know a thing about commerce, so I don't wanna bother them.

t_ch: 反倒是流浪者那边——据我所知，他们中的大部分都步入正轨了，但也免不了有些混球想借这个机会发笔横财。
t_en: But the vagrants — most of them are on the right track as far as I know, but naturally there're a few ne'er-do-wells who are trying to line their pockets with ill-gotten gains.

t_ch: 我现在就专注于教训那些家伙。得让他们明白，不论处于什么时期，干偷鸡摸狗的勾当都是会被惩罚的。
t_en: I'm just focusing on teaching those guys a lesson. Gotta make them understand that if they break the law, they're gonna be punished — no matter what era we're living in.

t_ch: 还在为「地火」工作吗？
t_en: Are you still working for Wildfire?

t_ch: 当然。从懂事起，帮「地火」做事就是我生活中最重要的部分。
t_en: Sure. Since I was a child, helping Wildfire has become the most important thing in my life.

t_ch: 我知道，「地火」的组建原本就是为了弥补银鬃铁卫的缺席，如果上下层区恢复了往常的样子，那「地火」也就没必要存在了。
t_en: I know that Wildfire was originally formed due to the absence of any Silvermane Guards. If things are back to normal between the Overworld and the Underworld, then there's no need for Wildfire to keep going on.

t_ch: 但…只要娜塔觉得「地火」还有存在的必要，那我就不会摘掉这条红绸带。
t_en: But... if Nat thinks there's still a need for Wildfire, then who am I to disagree?

t_ch: 先前的那场战斗…
t_en: Your last battle...

t_ch: 哪场？哦——你是说和可可利亚的战斗？
t_en: Which one? Oh — you mean the fight with Cocolia?

t_ch: 说来奇怪，有关那场战斗的细节我已经记不太清了。我只记得你被那个女人的武器贯穿，然后又神奇地站了起来……
t_en: Weirdly enough, I can't remember any details from that fight. I just remember you getting impaled by that woman's weapon, and then miraculously getting straight up again...

t_ch: 老实说，我完全没搞懂那时发生了什么。不过你居然有原地起死回生的能力，仔细想想还真是方便啊……
t_en: To be honest, I have no idea what was going on then. But it's incredible to think of you just coming back from the dead like that...

t_ch: 其实那不是我的能力……
t_en: Actually, that wasn't any power of mine...

t_ch: 不是吗？那你到底是怎么……
t_en: No? Then how did you do it...

t_ch: 欸，打住打住——你是不是又要说什么「星神」、「命途」之类的玩意了？我可没兴趣啊，还是留着下次再说吧。
t_en: Ah, stop it — don't give me that fluff about "Aeons" and "Paths" again. I'm not interested. Save it for next time.

t_ch: 没错，我就是这么强。
t_en: That's right. I have the power!

t_ch: 嚯，自信真好。不过你也确实有吹牛的资本。
t_en: Well, it's good to have confidence. But I suppose in this case, you really do have bragging rights.

t_ch: 要不是最近都快忙不过来了，我真想抽空跟你切磋切磋，交流一下作战技巧——以后总有机会的，你觉得呢？
t_en: I would really wanna take some time to learn from you and trade combat skills if I weren't so busy — but we'll get our chance some day. Right?

t_ch: 先聊到这儿吧。
t_en: Good chatting with you.

t_ch: 下次见，希儿。
t_en: See you next time, Seele.

t_ch: 下次见，替我跟三月和丹恒问好。
t_en: So long. Say hello to March and Dan Heng for me.

t_ch: 这里是我的家，我唯一熟悉的地方。有机会的话，我也想去见识见识外面的广阔世界…但现在还不是时候。
t_en: This is my home, the only place I've ever known. If I get the chance, I'd love to see the world out there... but now's not the time.

t_ch: 这个世界需要时间恢复，娜塔和布洛妮娅也需要我帮忙。我必须陪在他们所有人身边，尽自己的一份力。
t_en: The world needs time to recover. Nat and Bronya need my help. I have to stay with them and do my part.

t_ch: 关于布洛妮娅的选择...
t_en: About Bronya's decision...

t_ch: 我就知道她会做出正确的决定。
t_en: I knew that she would make the right choice.

t_ch: 星球没有被抢走，也没有更多人受伤——皆大欢喜，是吧？
t_en: Our planet was not taken over, and no more people were harmed... All's well that ends well, right?

t_ch: 皆大欢喜，皆大欢喜。
t_en: Indeed, all's well that ends well.

t_ch: 你看上去还没释怀。
t_en: You look like it's still weighing on your mind.

t_ch: …被你看出来了啊。的确，我脑子里一直在复盘这整件事……
t_en: ...You can see right through me. Yep, my mind has been going over this whole thing non-stop...

t_ch: 我原本以为，没了「星核」的祸害，这里的日子肯定会一天比一天好过。但现实和设想却差了好远。
t_en: I originally thought that without the Stellaron's destruction, life here would just get better by the day. However, the reality was far from how I imagined.

t_ch: 过去，任何问题似乎都可以靠拳头解决——不管发生了什么，我手里这柄镰刀就是硬道理。
t_en: In the past, it seemed that you could solve any problem with your fists... No matter what happened, the scythe in my hand made the rules.

t_ch: 但拿这次的事来说，面对不能靠蛮力战胜的对手…我不擅长处理这种问题，和人交涉、互相算计什么的…这对我来说太难了。
t_en: But just look at what happened this time. The enemy can't be beaten with brute force... I'm no good at things like this. Negotiating with people, plotting against each other... This is all just too difficult for me.

t_ch: 老实说，我会感觉有点迷茫…到底该怎么做才能算是帮到了布洛妮娅，帮到了贝洛伯格的大家？
t_en: To be honest, I feel a little lost... what am I supposed to do to help Bronya and the people of Belobog?

t_ch: 我要学的东西实在太多了。除了继续锻炼变强，我还得用其它方式武装自己。
t_en: There's just too much that I need to learn. I have to arm myself in other ways, not just through training and getting stronger.

t_ch: 冷静的头脑是最强大的武器。
t_en: A calm and composed mind is the most powerful weapon.

t_ch: 这话怎么听着这么耳熟…啊，想起来了！我最近在读的《斯维特兰娜战术集》里有一句话，好像和你说的是一个意思。
t_en: Why does that phrase sound familiar... Ah, I remember! There was a quote in "Svetlana's Stratagems" that I was reading recently, I think it said the same thing.

t_ch: 不必给自己那么大压力。
t_en: There's no need to put so much pressure on yourself.

t_ch: 呵，倒不是压力——我只是觉得事到如今，学习已经成了我必须尽到的责任。
t_en: Hah, that's no pressure! I just think that with the way things are now, studying is a responsibility that I must accept.

t_ch: 我最近在读一本叫《斯维特兰娜战术集》的书，军事相关的——里面有一句话让我印象深刻。
t_en: I've recently been reading a book called "Svetlana's Stratagems" about military strategy... There was one phrase in there that left a deep impression on me.

t_ch: 「在任何时候都能保持冷静的、批判性的思维方式，不被时而涌现的情绪蒙蔽，是身为将领最基本、却也是最难得的品质。」
t_en: "Being able to remain calm and maintain critical thought at any time, without allowing bursts of emotions to blind you, is the most basic, yet also the most rare quality of a leader."

t_ch: …我离书里说的境界还差得很远。之前在大矿区的时候，我果然还是被一时的火气给左右了…要不然，我说不定还能从那些黑衣人嘴里套出更多信息。
t_en: ...I am still far from the standard this book sets out. When I was in the Great Mine, I was thrown off by a moment of rage... If I had stayed cool, I might have been able to get a little more information from those people in black.

t_ch: 你已经成长很多了，希儿。
t_en: You have already grown a lot, Seele.

t_ch: 你当真这么觉得？不是单纯在顺着我的话往下说？
t_en: Do you really think so? You're not just trying to appease me?

t_ch: 贝洛伯格能有你是种幸运。
t_en: Belobog is lucky to have you.

t_ch: 怎、怎么突然这么一本正经地夸人，怪不像你的……
t_en: Wh—What are you doing? You can't just randomly praise people like that. It's not like you...

t_ch: …呵，算了，反正我要做的事都是一样。在下层区荒废的这些年，我要靠加倍…不，三倍的努力给追回来，至少不能被那群铁卫正规军给看扁了！
t_en: ...Hah, forget it. What I have to do remains the same. I have to double... no, triple my efforts to get back all those years I wasted in the Underworld. I can't allow myself to be underestimated by those official Silvermane Guards!

t_ch: 毕竟我答应了布洛妮娅，要和她一起把守那个秘密——要是我不到半程就掉队，压在她身上的担子可就太重了。
t_en: After all, I promised Bronya that I would protect that secret with her... If I were to drop out halfway, the burden would be too heavy for her to carry alone.

t_ch: 话说回来，那些黑衣人，他们虽然惹出不少乱子，但一个个的好像都挺见多识广的……
t_en: Having said that, those people in black clothes... though they've caused a lot of trouble, they all seem pretty educated...

t_ch: …嗯，就这么决定了。从今天起，我要多跟那些外派的公司员工交流，看看能不能从他们身上学到些有用的技能。
t_en: ...Alright, I've decided. From today forth, I will communicate more with those external IPC workers and see if I can learn any useful skills from them.

t_ch: 希望你们的探索有所进展，{NICKNAME}，也希望病痛远离你们的旅程。
t_en: I hope your exploration will make progress, {NICKNAME}, and your journey will be free of pain.

t_ch: 当然，就算不小心受了伤也不用慌张，全都交给医生就好了。
t_en: Of course, even if you get injured by accident, no need to panic. Just leave it to the doctor.

t_ch: 再见，娜塔莎。
t_en: Farewell, Natasha.

t_ch: 又见面了，{NICKNAME}。别来无恙？
t_en: We meet again, {NICKNAME}. How have you been?

t_ch: 托你们的福，这里的人们重新找回了希望。和上层的贸易渠道重新打通以后，很多此前浑浑度日的人也重拾了生活的动力。
t_en: With your help, the people here have regained hope. Reopening trade routes with the Overworld has given life new meaning for people who were once struggling just to get by.

t_ch: 尽管还有很多问题有待解决…但这无疑是个美妙的开端。这些都要归功于你，开拓者。
t_en: Even though there're still many things to fix... it sure is a great start. And it's all thanks to you, Trailblazer.

t_ch: 来聊聊吧，娜塔莎。
t_en: Let's talk, Natasha.

t_ch: 想和我聊聊？随时欢迎。
t_en: You want to talk with me? Any time.

t_ch: 下层区的大家过得如何？
t_en: How's everyone going down in the Underworld?

t_ch: 忙碌，充实。并不是每个人都能一下适应生活中发生的剧变，或许也有人的生意会因为封锁解除受到影响……
t_en: Busy, and well. Not everyone can just get used to all the changes so fast, and some businesses might be impacted by the lifting of the blockade...

t_ch: 但我没有听到什么抱怨的声音。我想，下层的每个人都知道自由来之不易，值得好好珍惜。
t_en: But I haven't heard any complaints at all. I think everyone in the Underworld knows that freedom is hard-earned — and worth cherishing.

t_ch: 你为他们…为我们重新点燃了信念。相信「只要付出汗水和努力，生活就一定会给我们报偿」的信念。
t_en: You've rekindled hope for them... for us. You know the saying: "As long as we put in the hard work, life will pay out dividends." I believe that.

t_ch: 最近病人多吗？
t_en: Have lots of people been getting sick?

t_ch: …诊所还是人满为患呢。虽然上层区的医疗设施更完备，人手也更充足……
t_en: ...The clinic's still packed with patients. Even though there're better medical facilities and more health staff up in the Overworlds...

t_ch: 但是大部分下层区人都付不起高昂的医疗费用。而且，据我所知…他们也不太信得过上层区的那些医生。
t_en: Most people down from the Underworld can't afford expensive medical bills. And as far as I know... They don't really trust those Overworld doctors.

t_ch: 我和妈妈又聊过几次，她会想办法为下层区居民提供就医支持。不过，对于那些已经习惯了我的治疗方式的患者来说…要做出改变大概很困难吧？
t_en: I've been talking it over with Mom, and she'll do her best to give medical support for the Underworld people. But will all those patients who're used to my style... be able to make that change?

t_ch: 有人的地方就摆脱不了病痛损伤的陪伴。只要我的手脚还能活动自如，我就会继续履行医生应尽的责任。
t_en: Wherever there are people, so there must be pain and sickness. As long as I can still move these arms and legs of mine, I'll keep performing my duty as a doctor.

t_ch: 关于你哥哥的事…
t_en: About your brother...

t_ch: 啊…关于瓦赫的事，我也想再次向你道谢。
t_en: Ah, Vache. I want to thank you again for that.

t_ch: 偶尔会有一种想法在我脑海中浮现——如果给我一个机会回到从前，我是不是有办法阻止哥哥误入歧途……
t_en: I sometimes get this thought popping up in my head — if I had the chance to go back in time, could I have stopped him from being led astray...

t_ch: 但类似的想法只会存在一瞬间。我们改变不了过去——在永远单向的人生旅程上，我们不断地选择、犯错…并为自己造成的后果担起责任。
t_en: But such thoughts only last for a second. We can't change the past — we're constantly making choices and mistakes on this one-way journey into the future that we call life... and also taking responsibility for our actions.

t_ch: 瓦赫为他犯下的罪恶付出了代价。万幸他在生命的最后没有继续沉沦，为这座城市留下了自己的研究结晶。
t_en: Vache has paid the price for his crimes. Fortunately, he'd turned things around before the end of his life, leaving his research behind for the good of the city.

t_ch: 好坏参半，这样的人生已经足够完整，我不必替他感到遗憾。
t_en: For better or for worse, such a life is so complete that I don't have to feel sorry for him anymore.

t_ch: 有关「地火」的未来…
t_en: About the future of Wildfire...

t_ch: 老实说，你们带来胜利的消息以后，我的第一反应就是…「地火」这些年来的任务终于结束了。
t_en: To be honest, after you brought news of the victory, my first reaction was... Wildfire's mission after all these years is finally over.

t_ch: 有些话我不会对满腔热情的年轻成员说——但对你，我可以完全坦诚。
t_en: There are some things I can't say to our younger members — but with you I can be completely candid.

t_ch: 维持下层区秩序的工作总有一天会被交还到银鬃铁卫手中，这是必然的结果。「地火」来自民间，最终也会归于民间。
t_en: The job of maintaining public order down in the Underworld will one day be handed back to the Silvermane Guards. Wildfire came out of the community, and will ultimately return to the community.

t_ch: 啊，听上去好像有些伤感呢。不过在未来可见的一段时间里，「地火」肯定还会活跃在下层区。毕竟，外面还有太多繁杂的事务需要有人处理。
t_en: Ah, that all sounds a little sad. However, Wildfire will remain active in the Underworld for the foreseeable future. After all, there's too much trouble out there that needs someone to help handle.

t_ch: 就先聊到这儿吧。
t_en: Nice chat.

t_ch: 和你聊天总是很开心，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: It's always a pleasure to talk with you, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 下次见，娜塔莎。
t_en: So long, Natasha.

t_ch: 再见，{NICKNAME}。诊所的大门永远都为你敞开。
t_en: Goodbye, {NICKNAME}. The doors of our clinic will always be wide open for you.

t_ch: 公司的处理你还满意吗？
t_en: Are you happy with how the IPC are handling things?

t_ch: 其实从公司决定介入这件事起，我们就没有多少选择的余地。如果不是那位托帕小姐高抬贵手，现在的局势想必会麻烦许多。
t_en: Honestly, ever since the IPC decided to get involved, we've ceased to have any freedom to make our own decisions. If it weren't for the mercy of Miss Topaz, the current situation would be a lot more dire.

t_ch: 面对强权不卑不亢、面对刁难据理力争，布洛妮娅已经证明了她作为大守护者的优秀品质，我对她的评价又涨了一分。
t_en: Unawed when faced with overwhelming power and malevolence, fighting to get any advantage she can — Bronya has proven her outstanding qualities as a Supreme Guardian. My opinion of her has only improved.

t_ch: 这颗星球需要时间，托帕为我们争取到的权益不一定长久。为了预防公司在未来的某天收回承诺，我们必须加快脚步。
t_en: This planet needs time. The rights that Topaz has fought to earn us may not last forever. We need to speed up our progress in case the IPC someday backtracks on its commitments.

t_ch: 不过，贝洛伯格的命运尚且掌握在它的孩子们手中，没有什么比这更值得欣慰的了。
t_en: Although, there is nothing more comforting than knowing that the fate of Belobog still lays in the hands of its children.

t_ch: 哟，这不是帮我修过东西的「外来者」嘛？
t_en: Hey, aren't you the outsider who helped me fix my stuff?

t_ch: 今天找我有什么事？机修、聊天或者听歌都可以哦。
t_en: What are you after today? Repairs, a chat, or just listening to music?

t_ch: 啊，难不成你下定决心要做我的助手了？欢迎欢迎～
t_en: Ah, so you've made up your mind about becoming my assistant, then? Welcome!

t_ch: 我只是想聊聊天…
t_en: I just want to chat...

t_ch: 哈哈，刚刚只是开玩笑。想聊些什么？
t_en: Ahaha, just kidding. What's up?

t_ch: 机械屋到底是做什么的？
t_en: What does the workshop actually do?

t_ch: 看看这装潢——想必你也能猜到了吧？这里当然是维修机械的店铺啦。
t_en: Just look at the decor — you can probably guess, right? It's a mechanical repairs shop, of course.

t_ch: 什么样的机械都能修？
t_en: You can fix all kinds of machines?

t_ch: 当然咯～这贝洛伯格可没有我修不好的东西。
t_en: Of course! There's nothing in Belobog that I can't fix.

t_ch: 小到地髓供暖器，大到汽车引擎，甚至是铁卫制式的自动机兵，对我来说都不成问题——啊，不过汽车和机兵车当然不能在这里修，有需要的话我会上门的。
t_en: Anything as small as a Geomarrow radiator to as large as a car engine — or even a Silvermane automaton — ah, but of course cars and automatons can't be dragged into this small room. I offer an outcall service for those.

t_ch: 另外，除了常见设备的维修翻新，机械屋还提供新功能附装的服务。比如，为推雪机增加外悬挂钻头、为烤面包机加装弹道计算装置……
t_en: As well as maintenance and renovations of everyday things, we also had a modding service — like fitting an external drill bit to a snowplow, or a ballistic computer to a toaster...

t_ch: 总之，我们机械屋的服务宗旨就是——没有我们做不到，只有你想不到！有什么需求就尽管来找我吧，姐姐可以给你个友情价哦。
t_en: Put simply, the motto of our workshop is: Nothing is unfixable, as long as you can imagine it! Come see us if there's anything we can do, we'll give you a friendly price.

t_ch: 平时生意怎么样？
t_en: How's business normally?

t_ch: 生意？那是什么？
t_en: Business? What's that?

t_ch: 哈哈哈，开个玩笑。我这机械屋的生意嘛，说多不多、说少不少，收入基本和我日常开销持平吧。
t_en: Haha, just kidding. There's not much to say about our workshop's business. I make about as much as we spend each day.

t_ch: 不过我也不打算卯着劲挣钱——人生苦短，这一辈子光挣钱有什么意思？有时间还不如多搞搞自己的兴趣爱好呢。
t_en: But I don't plan on just working for a crust — life is short, so what's the point of just living to make money? If I've got the time, I'd rather be tinkering on my hobbies.

t_ch: 啊，说到这个…我们机械屋偶尔也会有「摇滚演出」——你要是感兴趣的话，不如来捧个场？
t_en: Ah, speaking of which... We sometimes put on rock gigs here at the workshop. Why don't you come check it out, if you like?

t_ch: 原来如此。
t_en: I see.

t_ch: 还想聊些什么吗？
t_en: What else would you like to chat about?

t_ch: 听说这里有演出？
t_en: I heard there are gigs here sometimes?

t_ch: 是啊，你听谁说的？那人真有眼光！
t_en: That's right. Who told you that? They must have great taste!

t_ch: 在这上层区，把我们的摇滚艺术称作「演出」的人可算不多…大多数人都管我们的表演叫「噪音」，哈哈。
t_en: Not many people up in the Overworld consider our rock art a "gig"... Most people just call it "noise," haha.

t_ch: 你们的乐队叫什么？
t_en: What's your band called?

t_ch: 「机械热潮」——怎么样，酷吧？
t_en: We're called Mechanical Fever — cool eh?

t_ch: 你们的乐队都有谁？
t_en: Who's in your band?

t_ch: 哈，这你可问到点子上啦。
t_en: Ha, you've gotten straight to the point.

t_ch: 吉他手兼主唱嘛，当然是我啦。键盘手是我们朗道家最小最可爱的妹妹——玲可。
t_en: Guitar and vocals are handled by me, of course. The keyboardist is the youngest and cutest of us Landaus — Lynx.

t_ch: 鼓手是银鬃铁卫的要员之一，名叫佩拉。她经常在外面做义工…最近应该是在广场上给小孩子当导游吧？
t_en: On drums is Pela, one of the Silvermane Guards. She's always out on duty... These days, she should be out in the plaza watching over the kids.

t_ch: 至于贝斯手，我们乐队暂时没有……
t_en: As for the bassist, we don't have one...

t_ch: 原因嘛，和可可利亚有点关系…具体的细节就不方便透露啦。
t_en: And it's got something to do with Cocolia... I can't really say more than that.

t_ch: 你们什么时候有演出？
t_en: When's the next gig?

t_ch: 演出？随缘咯～想起来就开，想不起来就算了…这就是摇滚的精神！
t_en: Gig? Whenever we want~ If we want it, we do it — otherwise, we don't. That's rock music!

t_ch: 我明白了。
t_en: I understand.

t_ch: 像你这样对我们乐队感兴趣的人还挺少见的，还想聊些什么吗？
t_en: It's pretty rare to have someone like you who's so interested in our band. What else would you like to chat about?

t_ch: 没什么想问的了。
t_en: That's all questions I have.

t_ch: 行，之后还想聊天的话我也随时有空。
t_en: Cool, I'll be free to chat later whenever you want.

t_ch: 我们改天再聊吧。
t_en: Let's chat some other time.

t_ch: 好啊，欢迎下次光临！
t_en: Alright, come back soon!

t_ch: 欢迎光临机械屋「永动」……
t_en: Welcome to the Neverwinter Workshop...

t_ch: …咦？这不是被大守护者通缉的「外来者」嘛！铁卫正满世界找你们呢！
t_en: ...Huh? Aren't you that outsider who's wanted by the Supreme Guardian!? The guards are out hunting all over the place for you!

t_ch: 我看你们就别大摇大摆地在外面闲逛了，先在我这机械屋避避风头吧。
t_en: I think you should stop strutting outside so brazenly, and keep your head down here in my workshop.

t_ch: 我只是想聊聊天…
t_en: I just want to chat...

t_ch: 这节骨眼上还想着聊天啊…不过我没什么意见——想聊什么？
t_en: Chatting at a time like this... But that's okay with me. What would you like to chat about?

t_ch: 出了这机械屋我可就没办法罩着你了，在外边千万要小心啊。
t_en: I can't protect you if you step out of the workshop, so please be utterly careful outside.

t_ch: 哟，这不是「开拓者」{NICKNAME}嘛。欢迎光临机械屋！
t_en: Oh hey, it's {NICKNAME} the Trailblazer. Welcome to the workshop!

t_ch: 估计你也不是来修东西的…啊，我这不是在抱怨，别误会～
t_en: I'll hazard a guess that you didn't come looking for repairs... I'm not complaining. Don't get me wrong.

t_ch: 看在咱俩的交情上，把机械屋当成自己家就好啦——累了就在这儿随便休息吧。
t_en: Based on our friendship, you can just treat the workshop like your second home — come have a rest any time you're feeling tired.

t_ch: 好啊，之后想聊天的话我也随时都在哦。
t_en: Alright. I'll be here any time if you want to chat later.

t_ch: 杰帕德状况还好吗？
t_en: How's Gepard doing?

t_ch: 啊，你是说铁卫禁区那件事吗？老弟他只受了些皮肉伤，没什么大碍，放心吧——他可是铁卫的戍卫官，没那么脆弱。
t_en: Oh, you mean the thing in the Silvermane Guard Restricted Zone? He just suffered a few flesh wounds, nothing serious. Don't worry — he's captain of the Silvermane Guards, so he isn't that fragile.

t_ch: 在你们深入北方的裂界之后，我和老弟带着铁卫又勉强抵挡了几波攻势，最后瞅准了时机才把禁区大门重新关上。
t_en: After you went north into the Fragmentum, Gepard and I held off several waves of attack with some Silvermane Guards. We finally took our chances and shut off the entrance to the restricted zone.

t_ch: 有一说一，那场面可真是太惊险了…怪物没完没了地从裂界里涌出来，冲向禁区——我差点以为我们要撑不住了，好在有老弟兜底……
t_en: To tell the truth, the whole thing was thrilling... Endless monsters pouring out from the Fragmentum, rushing to the restricted zone. I almost thought we weren't gonna make it, but luckily I had my best guy with me...

t_ch: 以前的裂界攻势可没这么凶猛。啊，是说我还在铁卫任职的时候。
t_en: The previous Fragmentum attacks were nowhere near this vicious. *sigh* I mean back when I was still in the Silvermane Guards.

t_ch: 现在这状况…真是难为老弟了。
t_en: Now though... Things are really tough for Gepard.

t_ch: 你以前也上过战场吗？
t_en: You've been in combat before?

t_ch: 算是吧——虽然我是技术部的人，但我的专攻方向是「裂界」和「星核」。想在这些课题上弄出些名堂，可不能每天只泡在实验室里。
t_en: Pretty much — I may be from the Technology Division, but my areas of expertise are Fragmentum and Stellarons. And you can't remain confined to the lab if you want to make a name for yourself in these fields.

t_ch: 你以前好像不是很担心杰帕德？
t_en: You didn't seem to worry much about Gepard before?

t_ch: 怎么可能，那可是我老弟呀。我这做姐姐的，不管嘴上怎么说，心里都还是会挂念着他的。
t_en: Not true. He's my little brother. And no matter what, as his big sister, I will always be worrying about him.

t_ch: 只是我以前总想「朗道家的人各有各的坚持」，再加上老弟他确实武艺高强…我也就说服自己不再去担心他了。
t_en: It's just that I've always thought every Landau has their own drive. And my brother's really good at martial arts... I convinced myself that I wouldn't have to worry about him.

t_ch: 可见过如今的裂界攻势之后，我的想法也有所转变……
t_en: But seeing how the Fragmentum attacks have been developing has changed my mind...

t_ch: …等老弟下次回来，我一定得拉上佩拉一起，给他好好打打气。
t_en: ...When he comes back next time, I'll definitely bring Pela along and we'll back him up.

t_ch: 真是辛苦你们了。
t_en: Thanks so much.

t_ch: 嗐，这有什么！朗道家绝大部分人都走上了军旅生涯，这种场面没看惯也听惯了。
t_en: No big deal! Most Landaus members work their ways into military careers, and we're used to this.

t_ch: 只要你们这趟裂界之行有所收获，我们的努力就算是没白费啦。
t_en: It's worth our time as long as you're making valuable gains from your trips to the Fragmentum.

t_ch: 茉莉小姐最近如何？
t_en: How's Molly?

t_ch: 茉莉这姑娘嘛，大大咧咧的。脾气来得快，去得也快…和我还挺像，哈哈。
t_en: Molly? That girl's so carefree, and her temper can flip like the wind... A little bit like me, haha.

t_ch: 前阵子那件事也没让她消沉多久，这两天又转头和我一起研究她的传家宝盒子了。
t_en: She wasn't depressed for long after that last thing, and she's been working with me the past two days on her heirloom box.

t_ch: 这不，我估计她一时半会儿也回不了家，正考虑要不要邀请她当我的助手呢。
t_en: Right? I guess she won't be able to go home for a while, so I'm thinking about asking her to become my assistant.

t_ch: 这姑娘手脚麻利，干起活来毫不含糊…难怪她在技术部任职的时候能一个人揽下来那么多脏活累活。
t_en: This girl is so nimble and good at her work... No wonder she was able to handle so much manual labor by herself over at the Technology Division.

t_ch: 而且，我能看得出来，她是真心喜欢机械工程。要是再让她回到技术部那种乌烟瘴气的地方去，那就太可惜了……
t_en: Moreover, I can tell she loves mechanical engineering. It'd be such a pity to send her back to that stuffy vibe over at the Technology Division...

t_ch: 茉莉小姐最近如何？
t_en: How's Molly?

t_ch: 你要是问这个，那我可就不困了啊，哈哈～
t_en: Now that's a question that's perked me up, haha~

t_ch: 我跟你说，我就知道自己的眼光准出不了问题——不是我夸张啊，茉莉她真是难得一遇的机械天才！
t_en: Let me tell you, I knew there was nothing wrong with my eye — I'm not exaggerating here when I say, Molly is a rare mechanical genius!

t_ch: ——天才！天才的含金量你懂吗？她和我实在是太有共同语言了！
t_en: — Genius! Do you know the value of a true genius? She and I have so much in common!

t_ch: 意思你也是天才？
t_en: Are you saying that you're a genius, too?

t_ch: 那可不——不管是机械、科研还是摇滚，我可是样样精通喔。
t_en: Of course! Be it mechanics, research, or rock music — I'm a genius at all of them!

t_ch: 她的能力强在哪里？
t_en: What's her specialty?

t_ch: 茉莉这姑娘干起活来毫不含糊呢，还对各种机械结构有自己独到的见解……
t_en: Molly's such a good worker, and she forms her own insight into various mechanical structures...

t_ch: 有些连我都犯难的问题，她三下五除二就解决了。你说她是不是天才？
t_en: Some things that even I have trouble with, she can resolve meticulously. If that's not genius, what is?

t_ch: 不过有一点还是挺可惜的——这姑娘好像对摇滚不太感兴趣……
t_en: But it's just a pity that this kid isn't into rock 'n' roll...

t_ch: …哈，下一个目标这不就有了吗！得想办法让茉莉爱上摇滚，看看她能不能胜任「机械热潮」的贝斯手……
t_en: ...Ha, looks like we have our next target to work towards, then! I have to find a way for Molly to fall in love with rock music, and see if she'll be the bassist for Mechanical Fever...

t_ch: 要不拿这件事写首歌吧。
t_en: Why don't you write a song about it?

t_ch: 哈哈，我们想到一块去了。
t_en: Haha, great minds.

t_ch: 其实，我最近正琢磨歌词呢——这种难得的好灵感，我可不会让它白白溜走！
t_en: Actually, I've been slogging through writing some lyrics recently. When inspiration strikes, I'll put it to good use!

t_ch: 你真的下定决心要离开了吗？
t_en: Have you really made up your mind to leave?

t_ch: 是啊，我做了好久思想准备呢。要是这会儿再反悔，那我不是亏大了嘛～
t_en: Yes, I've been preparing myself mentally for a long time. If I back out now, it'll be a huge loss.

t_ch: 你舍得让杰帕德一个人留着吗？
t_en: Can you bear to leave Gepard here by himself?

t_ch: 嗯…如果我说舍得，你会信吗？
t_en: Well... would you believe me if I said yes?

t_ch: 哈哈，那可是我亲生弟弟啊，怎么会舍得扔下他一个人走呢？不过…我也别无选择就是了。
t_en: Haha, he is my brother by blood after all. How can I leave him all alone? But... I don't have any other choice.

t_ch: 话说回来，我又不是永远不回来了。再者，实在想他的话…我发信息骚扰他不就好了？
t_en: Also, i—it's not like I'm never coming back. And if I really miss him... I can just pester the heck outta him over the phone, right?

t_ch: 想联系的话总有办法啦，不用担心！
t_en: There's always a way for me to contact him if I need to, so don't worry!

t_ch: 你想离开的原因应该没那么简单吧？
t_en: The reason you want to leave isn't so simple, is it?

t_ch: 嗯…还是被你看出来了。
t_en: Ah... you can see right through me.

t_ch: 不过，这其中的原因我并不想和人说…还希望你能多理解一下啦。
t_en: I'm sorry, I'm not ready to talk to anyone about the reasons behind my decision... I hope you can understand.

t_ch: 好吧，我尊重你的选择。
t_en: Okay, I respect your choice.

t_ch: 哈哈，谢谢你。今后也请你多关照啦。
t_en: Haha, thank you. Make sure you look after me now~

t_ch: 最近还好吗？
t_en: How are things these days?

t_ch: 嗯？怎么突然问这个？
t_en: Hmm? Why are you asking me that all of a sudden?

t_ch: …啊，你是说之前在永冬岭发生的事吧？
t_en: ...Ah, you mean that thing back at Everwinter Hill?

t_ch: 狠话也放了，吉他也砸了…我要是再不从阴影里走出来，那可就太不摇滚了——你说是吧？
t_en: I did the growly voice and smashed up my guitar... It wouldn't be very rock 'n' roll if we just hid in the shadows and said nothing, right?

t_ch: 家庭也好，友情也好…面对挫折，以前的我只知道逃避，从没考虑过该怎么从根本上解决问题。
t_en: Whether it's family or friendships... In the face of setbacks, all I used to know how to do was escape. I never thought about trying to fix the root of the matter.

t_ch: 但现在不一样了——多亏了你和老弟的支持，我终于下定决心改变自己了。
t_en: But everything's different now — thanks to you and my bro's support, I've finally made the decision to make myself better.

t_ch: 对于贝洛伯格这座城市来说，我掌握的知识还能派上不少用场。我要用自己的能力去多帮助周围的人，把逃离这里的念头暂且放一放。
t_en: As for the city of Belobog, I can put my knowledge to lots of different uses. I'll use my talents to help people around me, and put aside for now any thoughts of escape.

t_ch: 不过话说回来，我对于旅行的兴趣也不是装出来的——希望等到时机成熟的那天，我能和你们一起踏上「开拓」之旅！
t_en: But then again, I'm not faking it when I say I love travel — I hope once the time's right that I can go out there and trailblaze together with you!

t_ch: 看来你在工地干得不错？
t_en: Looks like work is coming along well?

t_ch: 哟，你什么时候变成知心{F#小妹妹}{M#小弟弟}了？居然还会关心我的职业体验。
t_en: Ah, when did you become such a caring {F#young lady}{M#young man}? It's nice that you'd express concern in my work.

t_ch: 说真的，这里确实挺适合我。过去我在机械屋修的都是些钟表、供暖器之类的小玩意。这么个大家伙，够我琢磨好久了。
t_en: Honestly, this place suits me very well. All I'd ever fix back in the workshop were trivial things like watches and heaters. A big guy like this is something that I can really mull over for a long time.

t_ch: 而且现在大伙可仰仗我了，每天来请教问题的工人比演唱会观众都多——虽然不太摇滚，但这感觉还真有点小爽。
t_en: Plus, everyone is seriously counting on me now. I get so many people asking me things every day. It's like a crowd at a concert! It may not be rock'n'roll, but it's kind of cool.

t_ch: 工程结束后有什么打算吗？
t_en: Do you have any plans for when construction is complete?

t_ch: 等大机器人修好之后，这颗星球也会重获新生吧？不过我可不打算待到那天。
t_en: When the giant robot is all fixed, this planet will be reborn, right? But I don't plan on sticking around here until then.

t_ch: 只要工程一步入正轨，我就把担子丢给佩拉，自己找个地方逍遥快活去。
t_en: As soon as construction gets on track, I'll hand the reins over to Pela and find a place to enjoy myself.

t_ch: 估计到那时候，我已经成为白发苍苍的老希露瓦了吧，哈哈哈~
t_en: I imagine by that time, I'll already be old-lady Serval with gray hair, hahaha~

t_ch: 上车的事，不改主意了？
t_en: You don't regret joining us?

t_ch: 啊...你放心，我的灵魂才不会被重力束缚呢。
t_en: Ah... Don't worry, my spirit can't be weighed down.

t_ch: 等地上的工作完成后，我还是可以想办法来一次星间旅行。指不定到时候就不是被你们载着，而是和你们并排前进了。
t_en: When the work on the ground is complete, I'll still be able to plan my own interstellar journey. Who knows, maybe by then you won't be carrying me, but advancing alongside me.

t_ch: 啊，是你们！谢谢你们为我和女儿做的一切…你们有什么事也请尽管提，我一定尽力帮忙！
t_en: Hey, it's you! Thank you for everything you've done for me and my daughter... If there's anything you need, please feel free to ask. I'll do my best to help.

t_ch: 来聊聊天吧…
t_en: Let's have a chat...

t_ch: 好啊，正好我手头没什么事。
t_en: No problem. I just so happen to be free right now.

t_ch: 回家看过了吗？
t_en: Have you been back home yet?

t_ch: 说来惭愧，暂时还没有…
t_en: Not yet...

t_ch: 不过我已经向上级请假回家探亲了。看在我解决了失控机器人问题的份上，应该可以批准我回去吧？
t_en: But I've applied for a few days off to visit my family, and I think it will be approved since I solved the Malfunctioning Robot problem.

t_ch: 这次回去，我可一定要向母女俩好好道歉才行…
t_en: Once I get back home, I must apologize to my wife and my daughter...

t_ch: 哎，突然还有点紧张，心里揪得慌…这好不容易回趟家，我怎么还害怕起来了呢？啊哈哈……
t_en: *Sigh* I'm getting a little nervous. My heart is beating so fast... Finally, I am going to get to visit them, but why am I so scared?

t_ch: 回去和莉拉好好聊聊天！
t_en: Enjoy your father-daughter time with Lila!

t_ch: 你说得对。她都已经长成大姑娘了，我都没能和她真正地谈过心…
t_en: You're right. She's a big girl now and we've never really talked...

t_ch: 嗐，我现在才觉得自己之前根本就是自找麻烦。有什么事情非得自己憋着，没准和家人聊一聊，问题就都解决了呢……
t_en: I feel like I was asking for trouble by keeping everything to myself. Maybe all those problems could have been solved if I had just talked to my family...

t_ch: 你也是，内心有什么困惑一定要和自己的亲友好好谈谈！可别像我一样憋在心里，差点耽误了一辈子啊。
t_en: You too should talk to your friends or family about any confusion you feel inside! Don't keep everything to yourself as I did. It almost ruined my life.

t_ch: 这就是过来人的经验，哈哈哈哈！
t_en: This is coming from an old man, who knows from experience. Hahahaha!

t_ch: 能吃上一顿家里的饭，是件幸事。
t_en: Eating a home-cooked meal is indeed a fortunate thing.

t_ch: 哎呦，说得我都有点馋了，哈哈哈。
t_en: Yes! You are making me hungry. Hahaha.

t_ch: 我妻子的厨艺…算不上多好。但这么长时间过去了，我对那味道有点古怪的家常菜反倒有些期待……
t_en: My wife's cooking skills... aren't great. But after all this time, I've come to really love and look forward to that weird-smelling home-cooked aroma...

t_ch: 将来要是有机会的话，一定请你来我家做客，尝尝她的手艺。哈哈哈！
t_en: You have to come to my place someday to try her cooking. Haha!

t_ch: 最近都在忙什么？
t_en: What have you been up to lately?

t_ch: 老样子：矿石封装检查，军备例行维护之类的。
t_en: Same old, same old. Ore pack inspection, maintaining armaments—stuff like that.

t_ch: 以前总觉得这些活实在没意思…现在终于想通了，干起活来也有劲了。
t_en: I used to think that these jobs were boring as heck... Now I've finally let that go and I find them more interesting.

t_ch: 这都多亏了你啊。要不是你帮我和宝贝女儿通了信，我现在怕还是浑浑噩噩的吧…真的非常感谢你！
t_en: It's all thanks to you. If you hadn't helped me communicate with my daughter, I would still be in a slump... Thank you so much!

t_ch: 最近有什么趣事吗？
t_en: What's interesting lately?

t_ch: 趣事？实话说，这禁区里的活计就那么些——该上前线的上前线，该搞后勤的搞后勤…内容基本都没啥变化。
t_en: Interesting? Honestly, nothing. Apart from logistics and front-line battles, there is not much going on in the restricted zone... Every day is basically the same.

t_ch: …啊，我突然想到一个。就前两天…那个邓恩，你认识不？就那个军阶挺高的年轻人，禁区里管事的那个——你知道他以前在乐队里做过吧？
t_en: ... Ah, I got one. Two days ago, Dunn... Do you know Dunn? The young man with a high rank, that's in charge of the restricted zone. You know he used to be in a band, right?

t_ch: 和他打过交道。
t_en: I've had dealings with him.

t_ch: 那是谁？
t_en: Who's that?

t_ch: …不认识？呃，关系不大，反正就是个玩过乐队的年轻军官。
t_en: ...You don't know? Huh, no big deal. He is just a young officer who once played in a band.

t_ch: 那我接着说。那天，戍卫官杰帕德来后方例行巡查。那个邓恩，平时有事没事就在军队里把弄他那把琴…结果你猜怎么着？
t_en: I'll go on. Two days ago, Dunn played his guitar as usual, and Officer Gepard came to the rear for a routine inspection... Guess what happened?

t_ch: ——他那天忘记把琴收起来了！
t_en: He forgot to put the guitar away that day!

t_ch: 军队里连弹琴都不让吗？
t_en: Music isn't allowed in the military?

t_ch: …这怎么了？
t_en: ...What's wrong with that?

t_ch: 禁区虽说不禁止铁卫自娱自乐，但值岗期间开小差可是要记大过的。
t_en: It's okay to entertain yourself in the restricted zone, but neglecting your responsibilities while on duty is a major offense.

t_ch: 不过嘛，他那小队里有个铁卫小伙，总是替他背黑锅——好像是叫萨克雷？总之，那天邓恩也把萨克雷推出去顶锅了。
t_en: There is a member in his squad who always takes the blame for him. His name is Zackery, I think? Anyway, that day, Dunn blamed it on Zackery again.

t_ch: 杰帕德看见琴，就问：「这琴是谁的？」刚说完，萨克雷就第一个站出来喊：「报告长官，是我的！」
t_en: When Gepard saw the guitar, he asked, "Whose is this?" Right after speaking, Zackery stepped forward and said: "Sir, it's mine!"

t_ch: 然后你猜怎么着？杰帕德居然让那小子当场来一段！
t_en: You'll never guess what happened next! Gepard asked the boy to play something, right there on the spot!

t_ch: 好家伙。
t_en: Oh boy.

t_ch: 那不是坏事了？
t_en: He got him there.

t_ch: 那可不！
t_en: That's right!

t_ch: 这下萨克雷露怯了呀，听人说，他被吓得两腿直打哆嗦，哈哈哈！
t_en: It scared the heck out of Zackery. I heard that his legs were shaking uncontrollably. Hahaha!

t_ch: 后来实在没办法，邓恩只好出来自首。不过好在杰帕德戍卫官宽宏大量，这事就算过去了。
t_en: So Dunn had to give himself up. Luckily, officer Gepard is a lenient man. He let it go.

t_ch: 不过我又听说了些传闻，说是这两人过去认识…嗐，谁知道呢！
t_en: I heard that they knew each other... Hmmm, who knows what's true!?

t_ch: 你接着忙吧。
t_en: I'll leave you to your work.

t_ch: 没事了，再见。
t_en: Bye.

t_ch: 有什么需要帮忙的吗？有的话就尽管说——我虽然官不大，但在这禁区里还是有些人脉的。
t_en: Anything I can help with? Just say the word — I may not hold a high office but I've still got my connections around the restricted zone.

t_ch: 最近过得怎么样？
t_en: How have things been?

t_ch: 最近我还在等请假流程审批呢。听说排在我前面的人还有不少……
t_en: I've still waiting to hear back about my leave application. I heard there are a lotta people ahead of me in the line...

t_ch: 不过最近我们这前线的压力小了很多，估计大家都想偷个闲回家看看吧。只是不知道几时能排到我啊……
t_en: But pressure on the front line has receded a lot these days, so everyone just wants to take a break and reunite with their families. I just have no idea how long it's gonna take until my turn...

t_ch: 前线发生什么变化了？
t_en: What's changed on the front line?

t_ch: 听说最近前线的怪物明显变少了…不过这都是听说啊。毕竟我只是个后勤，根本没上过战场。
t_en: Well, I heard the monsters on the front line have dwindled recently... But that's just some people talking. I'm just a logistics guy, I've never set foot on the battleground before.

t_ch: 不过这里的气氛最近确实变了。以前战事吃紧的时候，这禁区里根本就是一副死气沉沉的样子。现在你去看，大家的精气神那是明显不一样……
t_en: But the vibe here has really changed. Back when things were more serious, this restricted zone used to seem so solemn. But now, just look — everyone's spirits are completely different...

t_ch: 挺好的，那祝你早日回家。
t_en: That's good then. I wish you a speedy return to your homelands.

t_ch: 祝你早日回家。
t_en: Hope you can get home soon.

t_ch: 哈哈，借你吉言！等我回了家，可得抓紧时间好好陪陪家人……
t_en: Haha, here's hoping! Once I get home, I'll have to catch up on spending time with the family...

t_ch: 冬城的孩子，「存护」骄傲的子民，抬起你们的头来！不要被忙碌且庸俗的日常磨平了棱角，时刻铭记筑城者和大守护者的教导！
t_en: Children of the Everwinter City, proud citizens of Preservation: Hold your heads up high! Grind NOT against the mundane vulgarities of your everyday lives. ALWAYS remember, deep in your hearts, the teachings of the Architects and the Supreme Guardian!

t_ch: （他好像非常投入…要不要听他说完呢？）
t_en: (He seems rather involved... Would you like to hear him finish his preaching?)

t_ch: 继续听多米尼克演讲…
t_en: Continue listening to Dominic...

t_ch: 你们心中存有疑虑，这很正常——因为你们左右张望，却看不到「存护」之所在；你们四处寻觅，却始终找不到「存护」存在的证据。
t_en: Normal it is, for you to have doubts in your hearts — because you look around yourselves, and you can't SEE Preservation anywhere; you cast your eyes hither and thither, and nary do you see ANY evidence of the very existence of Preservation.

t_ch: ——而我今天将解开诸位的疑虑，让你们切身感受到「存护」恩典的实在，让你们叹服筑城意志的伟大……
t_en: ...And yet comes this very day, I shall resolve your doubts and let you feel the true grace of Preservation, let you marvel at the almighty will of the Architects...

t_ch: 继续听…
t_en: Keep listening...

t_ch: …「存护」是克里珀精神的具现，它无时无刻不与我们同在。它于无形中塑造着我们的人格，影响着我们生命中的重要决策——
t_en: ...Preservation is the embodiment of Qlipoth, and is with us all the time. It's the secret force that imperceptibly shapes our personalities, impacting all the important decisions that we make during our lives...

t_ch: ——你之所以无法寻见它的踪影，是因为「存护」的恩典早已与我们熟悉的世界融为一体：它弥散于空气，夹带于光明，自人类降生之际便温柔地将它裹于怀中。
t_en: ...The reason why you struggle so to find it, is because Preservation's grace is integrated with the world we know, diffused throughout the air we breathe, refracted through the light we see. It has gently embraced us in its bosom since the very dawn of our existence.

t_ch: 大守护者的训诫提醒我们，绝不要将「存护」的恩典视作理所当然！我们必须继承筑城者先祖们的精神，在这个世界上坚韧地生存，与一切灾祸勇敢抗争！
t_en: The Admonishments of the Supreme Guardian remind us to never take Preservation's grace for granted! We must inherit the spirit of our Architect ancestors: To persevere and survive in this world of ours, and to bravely defy any calamities we face!

t_ch: 「裂界」、「寒潮」——不过是贯彻「存护」意志路上的小小磨难！兄弟姐妹们，和筑城者站在一起，和大守护者站在一起！追随她的步伐——我们注定胜利！
t_en: The Fragmentum and the Eternal Freeze — These are just bumps in the road on the path to carrying out the will of Preservation! O brothers and sisters, stand together with thine Architects and our Supreme Guardian! Follow in her footsteps — for victory is our destiny!

t_ch: 听听围观的人们在议论什么。
t_en: Listen to what the crowd is saying.

t_ch: 听听，多么慷慨激昂的演讲！赞美这位筑城者，他的言语给我黯淡的生命重新注入了信心……
t_en: Hear that? What an impassioned speech! Praise the Architects, as his words have reinvigorated my lust for life...

t_ch: …有你说的那么夸张吗？要我说，这些筑城者天天就会说些假大空的话——有时间搞这些表面功夫，不如想想怎么改善大伙的生活条件……
t_en: ...Is it really as great as you say? If you ask me, these Architects just say the same empty rhetoric day in day out — and if you've got time for all this bluster, you've got time to help improve everyone's living conditions around here...

t_ch: 存护！好耶！
t_en: Preservation! O praise!

t_ch: 我都走了一圈回来了，怎么还没结束啊…现在的年轻人，话可真多。
t_en: I've been off for a big walk, and come back. It's still going!? Kids these days, they never shut up.

t_ch: 就听到这里吧。
t_en: That's enough for me.

t_ch: 离开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: ——记住！「存护」无所不在，你迈出的每一小步都在祂的注视之下！
t_en: ...Remember! Preservation is everywhere around us. Every little step you take is under THEIR watch!

t_ch: 牢记你心中的道德律法，将筑城者的意志贯彻到生活的点点滴滴当中！永远、永远不要辜负伟大的克里珀！
t_en: Remember the moral law in your heart, and carry out the will of the Architects in each and every part of your life! Never, ever fail the almighty Qlipoth!

t_ch: 冬城的孩子，「存护」骄傲的子民，抬起你们的头来！不要被忙碌且庸俗的日常磨平了棱角，时刻铭记筑城者和大守护者的教导！
t_en: Children of the Everwinter City, proud citizens of Preservation: Hold your heads up high! Grind NOT against the mundane vulgarities of your everyday lives. ALWAYS remember, deep in your hearts, the teachings of the Architects and the Supreme Guardian!

t_ch: 眼前的男人醉心于他精心编织的演讲内容，似乎完全没有意识到你的到来。
t_en: The man before you seems so engrossed in the elaborate speech performance that he doesn't seem to have noticed your arrival.

t_ch: 大守护者换人了，你怎么看？
t_en: The Supreme Guardian has been replaced. What do you think?

t_ch: …这位听众，打断他人的演讲是极不礼貌的行为——我作为筑城者的代表，必须对你表示谴责！
t_en: ...This congregation member... Interrupting a sermon is extremely rude — As a representative of the Architects, I must express their condemnation!

t_ch: 但针对你的合理提问，我还是会给出公正的回答：布洛妮娅大人是「存护」意志忠诚的追随者，她将引领我们走向琥珀色的温暖黎明！
t_en: But I'll still give your reasonable question a fair answer: Lady Bronya is a loyal follower of the will of Preservation. She will lead us to its warm amber dawn!

t_ch: 可可利亚大人的牺牲令人痛心。她用一生的时间对抗「存护」的敌人…即使不被理解，即使遭受非议，她的内心仍然牵挂着人民，她的牺牲将被世代铭记！
t_en: Madam Cocolia's sacrifice was heartbreaking. She spent her whole life fighting against enemies of Preservation... Even if she was misunderstood and sometimes criticized, her heart still cares about the people. Her sacrifice will be remembered for generations!

t_ch: 由这样一位伟大的守护者挑选、培养出来的接班人，注定不会令人民、令筑城者失望。赞美「存护」，赞美布洛妮娅大人！
t_en: A successor hand-selected and trained by the Supreme Guardian is destined not to disappoint the people or the Architects. Praise Preservation, and praise Lady Bronya!

t_ch: 你来了！虽然我们之前交过手，但还是请允许我重新自我介绍一下吧。
t_en: You came! Even though we've faced off against each other before, please allow me to re-introduce myself.

t_ch: 我叫邓恩，直属于杰帕德戍卫官，目前担任这片军事禁区的总管。
t_en: My name's Dunn, and I serve under Captain Gepard. And I'm the current head of this military restricted zone.

t_ch: 杰帕德大人已经吩咐过我，允许各位「外来者」在禁区中自由活动。
t_en: Gepard's already instructed me to give Outsiders like yourselves free access here in the restricted zone.

t_ch: 如果你们在这禁区中遇到问题就告诉我，我会为你们出面解决的。
t_en: If you get into any trouble around here, let me know and I'll remedy it for you.

t_ch: 对不起，之前下手狠了点…
t_en: I apologize if I was a little rough last time...

t_ch: 你是说跟希露瓦一起擅闯禁区那件事？
t_en: Are you talking about that time you and Serval broke into the restricted zone?

t_ch: 不提也罢…我当时只是在执行命令而已。既然杰帕德戍卫官已经承认了你们的身份，那我自然也没有再刁难的必要了。
t_en: Let's not mention it... I was just carrying out my orders at the time. Since Captain Gepard has already acknowledged your identities, naturally, I didn't have to make things difficult back then.

t_ch: 不过当时我真的以为你们要下死手…唉，希露瓦她…真是狠心……
t_en: But at that time I really thought you guys were going to kill me... *sigh* Serval... Can't believe she'd have the heart to do that to me...

t_ch: 咳，没什么，这事就让它过去吧。这就叫不打不相识，是吧？
t_en: Anyway, let bygones be bygones. That fight actually made us closer, right?

t_ch: 有什么要我帮忙的吗？
t_en: Is there anything I can help you with?

t_ch: 哈哈，暂时没有。如果有的话，我会主动联系你的。
t_en: Haha, not right now. If I do, I'll get right in touch with you.

t_ch: …你要报复我了？
t_en: ...Are you taking revenge on me?

t_ch: 你误会了，我的意思是你身手不凡，将来或许还能帮上我们铁卫的忙……
t_en: You misunderstood me. I mean since you have extraordinary combat skills, in the future maybe you can help us Guards out...

t_ch: 请多关照！
t_en: Great seeing you!

t_ch: 哈哈，请多关照。
t_en: Haha, good to see you.

t_ch: 好了，聊了这么多，我也该回去工作了…你还有什么问题吗？我会尽可能为你解答。
t_en: Alright, after all this chit-chat, it's time for me to get back to work... Anything else you'd like to ask? I'll do my best to answer.

t_ch: 来聊聊天吧…
t_en: Let's have a chat...

t_ch: 好啊，你想聊点什么？
t_en: Alright, what would you like to chat about?

t_ch: 听说你也玩摇滚？
t_en: I heard you also do a bit of rock and roll?

t_ch: 哈哈，那都是我在士官学校读书时的事情了…虽然说我们是军官预备役，但大家毕竟都还是学生。精力过剩嘛，只好课后搞点兴趣爱好发泄一下了。
t_en: Haha, it all happened when I was still in cadet school... Although we were reserve officers, we were still students at the end of the day. So we needed some after-school hobbies to spend our excess energy.

t_ch: 我和几个志同道合的朋友就这么组成了摇滚乐队，当时在军官学校还算小有名气吧。
t_en: I formed a rock band with a few like-minded friends, which was quite famous in the cadet school back then.

t_ch: 你们乐队都有谁？
t_en: Who was in your band?

t_ch: 希露瓦，她是我们的吉他手兼主唱。她真的很有魅力，既潇洒又有才华，当之无愧。
t_en: Serval, she's our guitarist and lead singer. She was absolutely charming and talented. She totally deserved her position.

t_ch: 我是她的键盘手…我们两人向来配合得相当好。我一直觉得我俩…咳，没事。
t_en: I was her keyboardist... and the two of us always worked quite well together. I always felt like we were... Ahem, nothing.

t_ch: 接下来就是鼓手了——这位可是个重量级人物！你要不猜猜是谁？给你点提示，这位也是银鬃铁卫的一员。
t_en: The next one is the drummer — this one will shock you! Do you want to guess who it is? Here's a hint — they're also a member of the Silvermane Guards.

t_ch: 难道是杰帕德？
t_en: Could it be Gepard?

t_ch: 呃…他确实是铁卫里的高官，但他和他姐老不对付了。
t_en: Uh... he's a high-ranking officer in the Guards for sure, but he doesn't get along with his sister.

t_ch: 难道是佩拉？
t_en: Could it be Pela?

t_ch: 这你都能猜到？她不会和你聊过这事吧？
t_en: What a guess! She didn't tell you beforehand, did she?

t_ch: 难道是可可利亚？
t_en: Could it be Cocolia?

t_ch: …谨言慎行，外来者！
t_en: ...Be careful with your words, outsider!

t_ch: 正确答案就是佩拉。想不到吧？这么个一丝不苟的情报官小姑娘，私下里竟然是个爆裂的摇滚鼓手…大概是工作压力太大了吧？
t_en: The correct answer is Pela. Hard to believe, right? The petite, meticulous intelligence officer is actually a drummer in a rock band... I guess there's too much pressure on the job?

t_ch: 我听外面的人说，佩拉和希露瓦现在还经常一块排练，演出。真怀念……
t_en: I heard that Pela and Serval still do band practice and live performances together regularly, just like old times...

t_ch: 你们乐队就三个人？
t_en: There were only three of you in the band?

t_ch: 不对，我记得还有另外一个人…是谁来着？一下居然想不起来……
t_en: No, I remember there was another person... Who was it again? I can't believe it, I actually can't remember who it was...

t_ch: 摇滚乐队不需要贝斯吗？
t_en: Doesn't a rock band need a bass player?

t_ch: 噢——对对对，就是贝斯！你这一说我才想起来，哈哈哈…可他叫什么来着？不对，这人是男是女的来着……
t_en: Ah—yes, yes, the bass player! I just remembered it after hearing it from you. Haha...but what was his name again? Wait, was the bass player a guy or a girl...

t_ch: 我不玩摇滚，不太懂。
t_en: I don't know much about rock bands, sorry.

t_ch: …算了，想不起来，我放弃了。忘了那人吧，不重要。
t_en: ...Eh, never mind. I just can't remember. Don't worry about that person, it's not important.

t_ch: 你对希露瓦…
t_en: You and Serval...

t_ch: 都是过去式了。有些时候放手才是最好的选择……
t_en: It's history already. Sometimes letting go is the best choice...

t_ch: 你在军队里是做什么工作的？
t_en: What kind of job did you do in the military?

t_ch: 我负责禁区后方的综合管理。军需、后勤、能源…这些都归我管。
t_en: I am responsible for the general management of the support lines in the restricted zone. Munitions, logistics, energy... all of these are under my control.

t_ch: 我还负责和前线的直接沟通。假如前线被攻破了…我和弟兄们就要组成最后一道防线，防止敌人渗透城市。
t_en: I am also responsible for direct communication with the front line. If the front line was breached... my boys and I would have to form the last line of defense to prevent the enemy from infiltrating the city.

t_ch: 听起来很危险。
t_en: That sounds dangerous.

t_ch: 幸好有杰帕德戍卫官在…类似的事还一次都没发生过。
t_en: Fortunately, we have Captain Gepard... So things like that have never happened.

t_ch: 听起来还算安全。
t_en: That doesn't sound very dangerous.

t_ch: 多亏了杰帕德戍卫官…至少到目前为止，还从来没有裂界怪物穿越禁区的记录。
t_en: It's all thanks to Captain Gepard... At least so far, there has never been a record of a Fragmentum monster getting out of the restricted zone.

t_ch: 没事了。
t_en: That's all.

t_ch: 回见！
t_en: Later!

t_ch: 我还有事，回头再聊。
t_en: I have other things to do. Talk to you later.

t_ch: 回见！
t_en: Later!

t_ch: 前线压力越来越大，现在必须得调整铁卫的战术策略了……
t_en: The pressure on the front lines is increasing, and now the strategy of the Silvermane Guards must be adjusted...

t_ch: 外来者，来聊聊吧——你现在方便吗？
t_en: Outsider, let's talk. Are you free right now?

t_ch: 最近过得怎么样？
t_en: How have things been?

t_ch: 你问的真是时候，杰帕德戍卫官不久前才发了通知，说是裂界活动的频率明显放缓了——禁区的铁卫总算有时间喘口气了。
t_en: Impeccable timing. Captain Gepard just sent out a bulletin saying that the frequency of Fragmentum activity has noticeably diminished — all the Silvermane Guards around the restricted zone can finally take a breather.

t_ch: 听说很多长期驻扎前线的军官这次都能申请到休假…真不容易啊，唉。
t_en: I heard many officers who were stationed on the front line for long periods can finally take their leaves... A long time coming, I'd say.

t_ch: 虽说我只是个后勤官，不知道你们之前强闯禁区到底是为了什么…但从结果来看，裂界活动的减弱八成跟你们有关吧？
t_en: I'm just an insignificant logistics officer. I don't claim to know why you broke into the restricted zone last time... But whatever happened, I'm guessing the cut in Fragmentum activity has an <unbreak>80%</unbreak> chance of being directly caused by you, right?

t_ch: 是啊，就是我们拯救了贝洛伯格。
t_en: Yeah, we're the ones who saved Belobog.

t_ch: 哈哈，我就知道…那看来我们能放假都多亏了你啊。感激不尽！
t_en: Ha, I knew it! It seems like we all owe you a debt of gratitude for our vacations. Thanks so much!

t_ch: 详情不便透露…
t_en: I can't say anything specific...

t_ch: 我懂，我懂…杰帕德长官也不让我们过多过问，这个话题就此打住吧。
t_en: Got it, got it... Captain Gepard doesn't like us asking too much either, so let's park this topic for now.

t_ch: 我就是没闯过军事禁区，想试试。
t_en: I've never busted into a restricted zone before, and wanna give it a try.

t_ch: 呃…你是在开玩笑，对吧？
t_en: Uh... You're kidding, right?

t_ch: 哎…只可惜可可利亚大人她…她为了守护贝洛伯格选择了牺牲自己。真是伟大。
t_en: *sigh* If only Madam Cocolia hadn't... Hadn't needed to sacrifice herself to save Belobog. What a noble woman.

t_ch: 希望布洛妮娅大人能继承她的意志……
t_en: I hope Lady Bronya can inherit her iron will...

t_ch: 公司的事已经顺利解决了。
t_en: The problem with the IPC has been taken care of.

t_ch: 是啊，我听说了。好在布洛妮娅大人没有签订那份协议。
t_en: Yeah, I heard. Thankfully Lady Bronya didn't sign that agreement.

t_ch: 虽然铁卫内部还是不太信任那些黑衣人，不过我认为，现在我们走在正确的道路上。
t_en: Although the Silvermane Guards still do not fully trust those people dressed in black, I believe that we are now on the correct path.

t_ch: 为什么这么说？
t_en: Why do you say that?

t_ch: 之前你态度挺激烈的。
t_en: You were quite worked up about it before.

t_ch: 我们当然不能接受黑衣人的不平等条约。但必须承认，没有他们的技术，贝洛伯格的复兴将异常艰难。
t_en: Naturally, we cannot accept an unequal treaty from those people dressed in black. However, I admit that without their technology, the revival of Belobog would be extremely difficult.

t_ch: 如今我们已经展现了重建家园的决心。这份合作是我们赢来的，不是他们的施舍。
t_en: Today we have shown our determination to rebuild our home. This cooperation was won by us. It was not their charity.

t_ch: 当然，还是没法排除他们单方面反悔的可能性。银鬃铁卫会严加监管这些人的动向。
t_en: Of course, we cannot rule out the possibility of them backing out of this. The Silvermane Guards will be monitoring their movements closely.

t_ch: 如果他们真的密谋对贝洛伯格不利，我会第一时间提醒大守护者的。
t_en: If they really are conspiring against Belobog, I will alert the Supreme Guardian immediately.

t_ch: 三百零一、三百零二……
t_en: Year <unbreak>301</unbreak>, Year <unbreak>302</unbreak>...

t_ch: 嗯，看来筑城<unbreak>232</unbreak>年的档案没有错漏。接下来该是……
t_en: Hmm, it seems like the archives of Fortification Year <unbreak>232</unbreak> are in order. The next should be...

t_ch: 是筑城<unbreak>233</unbreak>年。
t_en: Fortification Year <unbreak>233</unbreak>.

t_ch: 没错，是筑城<unbreak>233</unbreak>年……
t_en: That's right. It's Fortification Year <unbreak>233</unbreak>.

t_ch: 你这是在干什么？
t_en: What are you doing?

t_ch: 先别打扰我，我正在整理档案……
t_en: Don't disturb me. I'm sorting out the archives...

t_ch: 佩~拉~姐~姐~
t_en: Miss~ Pela~

t_ch: 呃，请个别大人……
t_en: Um, please don't...

t_ch: …咿？！你什么时候冒出来的！
t_en: ...Eh? When did you get here?

t_ch: 我刚进来没多久。
t_en: I just got here.

t_ch: 那能请你别悄无声息地站在人身后，然后又冷不丁吓人一跳吗？
t_en: Could you not stand behind people so quietly like that? It's kinda creepy!

t_ch: 哈，吓到你了吧！
t_en: Hah, did I give you a fright?

t_ch: 啊啊…！真是的，幼不幼稚啊——你都多大个人了！
t_en: Argh! How old are you? Stop acting like a kid!

t_ch: 要帮你整理书架上面的档案吗？
t_en: Do you need help in organizing the archives on the bookshelf?

t_ch: …我能够得着。谢谢你关心，下次别关心了。
t_en: ...I can reach them. Thanks for your concern, but please don't do that again.

t_ch: 呃，我刚刚清点到哪一年了来着……
t_en: Um, which year was it just now?

t_ch: 哎，算了…收拾了一整天，顺势休息会也好。
t_en: Alas, forget it... It's time I take a break after all the work.

t_ch: 我就陪你聊聊天吧。找我有什么事吗？
t_en: I'll keep you company then. What can I do for you?

t_ch: 我想聊聊…
t_en: I'd like to chat with you...

t_ch: 情报官的工作到底是什么？
t_en: What does an Intelligence Officer do?

t_ch: 搜集战场情报，将其中有用的信息逐条析出，整理好之后便能用于指导银鬃铁卫的大小战略战术了。
t_en: I collect battlefield intelligence, extract useful information, and sort them out. The information will be used to formulate strategies and tactics for the Silvermane Guards.

t_ch: 除了作战方面的事，我平时也会协助杰帕德整顿军务——简单总结，都是一些涉及脑力的工作。
t_en: Besides assisting work at the frontline, I also help Gepard to manage military affairs. In other words, my job involves a lot of thinking.

t_ch: 听起来很辛苦。
t_en: It sounds challenging.

t_ch: 工作本身倒没多辛苦。不如说，从杂乱的信息中梳理出有序的部分反而会令我感到快乐。
t_en: It isn't as tough as it sounds. Rather, I actually take pleasure in making things in order from a mess.

t_ch: 听起来很轻松。
t_en: It sounds easy.

t_ch: 确实，我也很享受这份工作——从杂乱的信息中梳理出秩序的部分，恐怕没有比这更让人愉悦的事情了。
t_en: Indeed, and I really enjoy my work. Nothing is more fun than making things in order from a mess.

t_ch: 听起来似乎不包括整理书架…
t_en: It sounds like your job scope doesn't include organizing bookshelves.

t_ch: 就知道你会这么说。整理书架当然不在铁卫情报官的工作职责范围内——这只是义工罢了。
t_en: I knew you would say that. Organizing bookshelves is certainly not part of my job — it's just volunteer work.

t_ch: 你能再多说说工作的细节吗？
t_en: Can you tell me the details about your job?

t_ch: 很遗憾，细节部分就属于银鬃铁卫…不，应该说是贝洛伯格的最高机密了喔。
t_en: Unfortunately, the details are the top secret of the Silvermane Guards — or more accurately — of Belobog.

t_ch: 你和杰帕德关系怎么样？
t_en: How's your relationship with Gepard?

t_ch: 杰帕德戍卫官是个非常可靠的人——仅指承担公职的部分。
t_en: Captain Gepard is a very reliable person... as far as carrying out his public duties is concerned.

t_ch: 其余的部分嘛…我只能用四个字评价：惨不忍睹。大到武器维护、小到仪容仪表，他身上能挑出的毛病简直比筛子眼还多。
t_en: As for the other aspects of his life, I can only say he is hopeless. There are so many things he can do better, be it maintaining his weapon or his appearance.

t_ch: 但神奇的是…即便邋遢成这样，他也能给希露瓦提出生活上的建议。那对姐弟的关系…连我这个情报官都无法准确解析啊。
t_en: Even as unkempt as he is, he is still, for some reason, able to give Serval life advice. The relationship between these siblings is puzzling even to an Intelligence Officer like myself.

t_ch: 你和希露瓦关系怎么样？
t_en: How's your relationship with Serval?

t_ch: 希露瓦当然是我的好闺蜜啦，我们还一起玩乐队呢。她是吉他手兼主唱，至于我嘛…其实是鼓手。
t_en: Serval is, of course, my best friend. We've even formed a band together. She's the guitarist and vocalist, while I'm the drummer.

t_ch: 平时下班后我也经常到她的机械屋里玩，如果玲可在的话我也会留下过夜。独居生活就是这样，一个人闷在家里总会无聊的。
t_en: I often visit her workshop after work. If Lynx is there, I'd stay the night. As I live alone, there are times I find it boring to stay at home.

t_ch: 好像没什么想聊的了。
t_en: That's all for now.

t_ch: 那就等你有心情了再聊吧。
t_en: Let's carry on later.

t_ch: 你接着忙吧。
t_en: I'll leave you to your work.

t_ch: 好，我也该开工了。
t_en: All right. I should get back to work too.

t_ch: 嗯…谁把《贝洛伯格大事年表》放在这里的？可别让我抓到了……
t_en: Who put this Belobog Chronicles here? You'd better not let me catch you...

t_ch: 哼，我就知道你在身后。别想再吓我一跳，你不会得逞的。
t_en: Hmph, I know you're standing behind me. I won't let you scare me this time.

t_ch: 说吧，找我有什么事吗？
t_en: So, what brings you here?

t_ch: 腐败铁卫的事情处理得如何？
t_en: How's the matter regarding the corrupt guards?

t_ch: 啊，那件事已经被妥善处理了。
t_en: Oh, that has been taken care of properly.

t_ch: 被抓到的那几个家伙只是小人物，那天他们招供完就被移交到铁卫军事法庭了。
t_en: Those captured are merely small fries. After making their confessions, they were sent to the Silvermane Guard Military Court to be court-martialed.

t_ch: 我已经安排了铁卫协助司法大臣继续侦办这起腐败案件。那位大臣是个手腕强硬的角色，估计要不了多久就能查个水落石出。
t_en: I've dispatched some guards to assist the Minister of Justice in the investigation of this corruption case. The minister handles cases with an iron fist, so I suppose it won't be long before they get to bottom of this.

t_ch: 总之…我们已经把该办的都办了，之后就没必要多操心啦。
t_en: Anyway... we've done everything we need to do. There's no need to worry about what happens next.

t_ch: 不愧是铁卫的情报官。
t_en: You're a great Intelligence Officer.

t_ch: 过奖了，这件事能顺利解决也多亏了你的协助，无论如何都要谢谢你才是。
t_en: Thanks for the compliment. Your help has played a crucial role in this matter. We really appreciate it.

t_ch: 不愧是我们！
t_en: We're amazing!

t_ch: 哈…你可真不谦虚，哪怕装装样子也行呀？
t_en: Haha... Modesty means nothing to you, huh?

t_ch: 不过嘛，这件事能顺利解决也多亏了你的协助，无论如何都要谢谢你才是。
t_en: Anyway, your help has played a crucial role in this matter. We really appreciate it.

t_ch: 只是，别忘了——能力越大责任越大——以后再有什么麻烦事的话，我可是会第一时间来找你的哦，哼哼。
t_en: Still, please bear in mind that with great power comes great responsibility. I'll come to you if I'm in trouble again, hehe.

t_ch: 茉莉小姐在那之后状态如何？
t_en: How's Molly after that incident?

t_ch: 呃，你这不应该直接问她嘛……
t_en: Um, shouldn't you ask her directly?

t_ch: 你说得有道理…
t_en: You have a point...

t_ch: 算了…反正我天天都去机械屋，现在和茉莉的关系也不错。
t_en: Forget it. I visit the workshop every day, and I've been on good terms with Molly.

t_ch: 我就和你简单说说吧，省得你操心。
t_en: I'll tell you how she's faring, so that you don't have to worry about her.

t_ch: 我和她没那么熟，不好意思问。
t_en: I don't know her that well, so I find it hard to ask her.

t_ch: 这么冷淡…她可是把你当救命恩人呢。
t_en: How indifferent. You should know that she sees you as her savior.

t_ch: 不过既然你这么关心她，我就和你简单说说吧。
t_en: But since you're so concerned about her, I'll tell you how she's faring.

t_ch: 我就想和你聊聊天嘛～
t_en: I just want to chat with you...
